hypothesis: Validated semantic hints may still be useful if applied more narrowly or only as candidate-priority metadata.

known_prior_result.conclusion: The first broad non-shadow semantic-router variant regressed; this does not disprove the entire semantic-router idea.

safety_controls: All variant runs write only isolated outputs under outputs/llm_semantic_router_feedback_loop/.
Generated diagnostic prompts are coverage-only and not promotion evidence.

variants.exact_isolated_change: Apply hints only for UNKNOWN, confidence < 0.35, or explicit ambiguous phrase plus weak schema relevance.
variants.hypothesis: The first broad non-shadow semantic-router variant regressed; narrower eligibility may preserve useful fallback behavior while avoiding high-confidence perturbations.

variants.exact_isolated_change: Allow route/domain/candidate priority changes but keep helper intent as metadata only.
variants.hypothesis: The first trial changed intent metadata without improving SQL/API/answer; recording intent but not applying it may remove noise.

variants.exact_isolated_change: Only prepend validated candidate tables/APIs. Do not change route_type, domain_type, or answer intent.
variants.hypothesis: Validated semantic hints may be useful as table/API priority signals without changing route, domain, or intent.

variants.exact_isolated_change: Apply validated hints only when deterministic domain_type is UNKNOWN.
variants.hypothesis: The helper may be safest as a true fallback only when deterministic routing cannot identify a domain.

variants.exact_isolated_change: Do not allow helper route changes into API_ONLY or SQL_THEN_API when the deterministic route is SQL_ONLY.
variants.hypothesis: Dry-run API behavior may hurt answers; semantic hints should not force API routes unless the existing route already expects API evidence.
